mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already above 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of historical cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflect supply-side constraints that force immediate equity repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns do not materialize in > 50% of triggered instances during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0% triggers a liquidity contraction that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals corporate profit margin compression, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2.0% QoQ while RSAFS growth < 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization improvements that boost corporate earnings forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 45% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0% YoY.
